header_summary:
The discussions from the Bitcoin Development Mailing List delve into several critical aspects of blockchain technology and its future resilience against quantum threats. The debate highlights discrepancies in the rescue protocols for different Bitcoin address types post-Quantum Day, underscoring that not all coins, including those in reused paper wallets, can be recovered due to lacking Knowledge Asymmetries (KAs) [Source URL](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/XeI-rNO4AO9UCO-CL4K8scZ8VZjc_5ctaY4sw98wepKsOgEQkpExB8eagrO_TqplOUpt9WERYxitzsoCaIjoz99M9mfrrIYMKkdMij1_Pls=@proton.me/T/#m9235a4ba8ca2f405ae7020cd1a45ee1878385c28).

Junhyuk Jun Lee raises concerns about the long-term economic implications for Bitcoin, questioning the sustainability of mining operations as block rewards diminish, projected to reach 0.195 BTC by 2040. This inquiry is part of a broader discussion on maintaining network security and miner incentives as subsidy reductions potentially alter the economic dynamics of Bitcoin [Source URL](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/CAKaEYh+t_sPhQaNL91_Pmwc9HdVqQjzXKMDUy_YpsaLCrJ1zhw@mail.gmail.com/T/#m493c8674a4abcad7b1806940c90671b6edd4254b).

The "Four Horsemen (of the Blockchain)" specification proposes an integrated approach to Bitcoin coinjoin protocols, aiming to enhance transaction privacy while addressing current system limitations. By combining technologies like WabiSabi, ZeroLink, JoinMarket, and PayJoin, this unified protocol seeks to optimize block space usage, reduce fees, and improve liquidity, demonstrating an evolving landscape for on-chain transaction privacy [Source URL](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/the-four-horsemen/2728).

Finally, the exploration of game-theory problems in Bitcoin’s post-quantum scenarios reveals potential strategic shifts due to quantum computing capabilities. Discussions include the possibility of a collusion between miners and quantum entities to exploit legacy coins, highlighting the need for robust, quantum-resistant strategies to secure the blockchain against such advanced threats [Source URL](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/CALZpt+FOUJF3E7YDk5xh-Cv9kxduGiuOPVK5x171=25C3ryJPQ@mail.gmail.com/T/#u#m2143c378036549c5d3d655b1e62399a738ccf14a).

recent_posts.title: Quantum Recovery Of Hashed Address Secured Coins With No Confiscatory Risk
recent_posts.summary: - Debate on Bitcoin’s UTXOs rescue post-Quantum Day questions full coverage feasibility.  - Concerns raised about potential CRQC threats from sharing xpubs risking coin theft.  - Discussion emphasizes understanding Knowledge Asymmetries and their management in protocols.

recent_posts.title: [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards: preventing miner deviation as the block rewards approaches zero
recent_posts.summary:
- Junhyuk Jun Lee questions predictive analysis on Bitcoin's subsidy reductions by 2040.
- Discussions aim to grasp long-term economic effects and viability due to halving events.
- A robust framework is essential for Bitcoin's sustainability amidst diminishing miner incentives.

recent_posts.title: The game-theory problems of PQ sunsetting modes
recent_posts.summary: - Game-theory analysis shows strategic impacts on blockchain security due to quantum computing.  - Strategic scenarios include collusions and economic incentives for blockchain manipulations.  - Research is essential to reassess security measures and protect blockchain in quantum advancements.

recent_posts.title: The Four Horsemen
recent_posts.summary:
- The specification seeks to enhance Bitcoin privacy and efficiency via a unified system.
- It integrates subprotocols like WabiSabi and PayJoin, each addressing specific transaction issues.
- Future improvements and community feedback are incorporated to refine user experience and privacy.

recent_posts.title: Babilonia - probabilistic coinjoin and covert betting
recent_posts.summary:
- The paper’s version 1.1 update enhances protocol message ordering and discusses new frameworks.
- Two approaches for offchain game or bet executions are proposed, one simpler than the other.
- Further enhancements may involve advanced technical features like PTLCs and ZKPs.

recent_posts.title: [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards : preventing miner's deviation when the Bitcoin rewards is zero
recent_posts.summary:
- Research explores blockchain dynamics, highlighting attacker and defender capabilities.
- Studies show economic limits affect strategic defense absence in blockchain security.
- Reactive defense significantly influences outcomes of security contests in blockchain models.

active_posts.title: Quantum Recovery Of Hashed Address Secured Coins With No Confiscatory Risk
active_posts.summary:
- Quantum computing threatens cryptocurrency security, necessitating quantum-safe encryption alternatives.
- Various recovery proposals lack a universal solution but offer mechanisms like hierarchical and stateful proofs.
- Non-hashed addresses remain vulnerable despite strategies like commit-reveal schemes to mitigate risks.

active_posts.title: [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards: preventing miner deviation as the block rewards approaches zero
active_posts.summary:
- Junhyuk Lee's paper discusses Bitcoin's security with no block rewards, focusing on miner behavior.
- It identifies a threshold where miners may deviate from honesty for profit maximization.
- Strategies like base fees and adaptive block sizing are proposed to maintain network integrity.

active_posts.title: The game-theory problems of PQ sunsetting modes
active_posts.summary:
- Game-theory explores strategic impacts of quantum scenarios on Bitcoin's security and economics.
- Collusion risks between quantum entities and miners could compromise blockchain via large-scale extractions.
- Research into quantum-resistant strategies is vital to safeguard blockchain integrity in a post-quantum era.

active_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal enhances BIP-360 by optimizing EC public key recoveries in P2MR structures.
- It introduces a recoverable EC leaf to shrink witness size and maintains quantum resistance.
- Changes to witness validation and nonce generation processes are also proposed to ensure security.

active_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin's privacy enhancements focus on sidechains, L2s, and wallet integrations.
- CISA may reduce costs for privacy methods like CoinJoin without increasing privacy.
- Regulatory and technical challenges limit major changes to Bitcoin's core protocol.

active_posts.title: Addressing the Diminishing Block Subsidy
active_posts.summary:
- The proposed 0.25 BTC tail emission per block aims to stabilize miner revenue by 2040.
- Critics fear exceeding the 21 million BTC cap could affect its value storage narrative.
- Flexibility in adjusting emissions could help maintain Bitcoin’s security without consensus issues.

today_in_history_posts.title: Covenant opcode proposal OP_CONSTRAINDESTINATION (an alternative to OP_CTV)
today_in_history_posts.summary: - A new opcode, OP_CONSTRAINDESTINATION, aims to restrict output destinations in Bitcoin scripts.  - Unlike OP_CHECKTEMPLATEVERIFY, OP_CONSTRAINDESTINATION offers transaction flexibility without added constraints.   - The proposal is available on Github for public feedback and improvement suggestions.

today_in_history_posts.title: Removing the Dust Limit
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Jeremy Rubin advocates removing Bitcoin's dust limit, citing privacy and utility benefits.
- Rubin suggests dust outputs could enhance smart contracts and value markers in transactions.
- He counters the spam and fingerprinting concerns, emphasizing potential preventive measures.

today_in_history_posts.title: Thoughts on scaling and consensus changes (2023)
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin scalability involves 50,000 off-chain "bitcoin banks" for weekly transactions.
- These bitcoin banks, using tools like Coinpools and Utreexo, aim to ensure security and affordability.
- Networked banks support auditability and regulatory compliance with tools ensuring safe operation.
